_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that people can interpret the same historical events in different ways, and why those differences matter.

_source.w: We are learning that people can interpret the same historical events in different ways, and why those differences matter.
y: Understanding different interpretations helps us think critically rather than accepting one version of events.
History is not always agreed upon — learning this helps us weigh evidence and form our own views.
Recognising contested interpretations is essential for understanding Australia's complex past.
eg: Discussing why Aboriginal Peoples describe British colonisation as an invasion.
Comparing how the words 'settlement' and 'invasion' change the way we think about 1788.
Looking at how different textbooks describe the same historical event in different ways.
Exploring why some people celebrate Australia Day while others see it as a day of mourning.
Discussing what 'contested' means in history and finding examples in our studies.
Talking about why it is important to hear more than one side of any story.
